LAW AND MOTION, 301, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, SUBMITTED ON MAR-12-02 BY JUDGE JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTER: ERIC THRONE.(301)
NOTICE OF PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM FORFEITURE; P & A; DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT HALPER, JANICE AKA JANICE HALPER HAYES HEARING SET FOR APR-10-2002 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, NOTICE OF PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM FORFEITURE. MOVING AND OPPOSING PARTIES APPEARED. ARGUED AND THE COURT DENIED DEFENDANT JANICE HALPER'S PETITION FOR RELIEF FROM FORFEITURE OF LEASE. MR. APPLEGATE REQUESTED A STAY OF EVICTION TO PURSUE THE APPELLATE PROCESS. THE COURT GRANTED A STAY TO APRIL 26TH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTED BY ERIC THRONE. CSR 7855

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petition For Relief From Forfeiture; P & A; Declaration
Off Calendar Apr-10-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Douglas A. Applegate, Esq. of SEILER EPSTEIN 788-8180 for defendant JANICE HALPER petitioning for relief from forfeiture of lease
Matthew K. Hindman, Esq. of ZITRIN & MASTROMONACO LLP 956-4030 for Plaintiff ONE THOUSANT GREEN CO., a California Corporation opposing party

Argued and the Court DENIED defendant JANICE HALPER's petition for relief from forfeiture of lease. Mr. Applegate requested a stay of eviction to pursue the appellate process. The Court granted a stay to April 26th. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
(JJM/md) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855
